_description: Passive fingerprint signatures for ASIC miners. Used only for identification, no authentication attempted.

firmware_extraction.note: Antminer stock firmware embeds date in page or via /cgi-bin/get_system_info.cgi
Whatsminer FW version often visible on status page

miners.note: UNVERIFIED: a GitHub code search for this exact CGI path returned zero hits outside our own repo, and the real whatsminer backend in 256foundation/asic-rs only implements the cgminer RPC protocol (port 4028), not an HTTP API — so this path may not exist. Title/body patterns (vendor name) are likely fine regardless. No live device available to test.

Replaced bare 'Avalon'/'avalon' (2026-07-20) — confirmed via GitHub search it collides with real unrelated projects (a JS MVVM framework called avalon.js, an Apache commons-jelly module) the same way EdgeOS/deviceModel and ESP32 did elsewhere in this file. Fetched the actual official Canaan-Creative/Avalon-Management-System repo's index.html: real title is 'Avalon Management System', which is what's here now — more specific (helps the specificity tiebreaker too) and still matches the real device via substring. Could not verify /cgi-bin/miner_status.cgi itself against a live device or authoritative source (asic-rs's avalonminer backend only implements the cgminer RPC protocol on 4028, not an HTTP API) — that part is still unverified.

firmware_extraction.note: The old /api/v1/status and /api/v1/overview paths don't appear in any real Braiins OS+ client or docs I could find. A real open-source Home Assistant integration (aleixps/Braiins-OS-HA) confirms the actual BOS+ management API lives under /api/v1/{auth/login, miner/details, configuration/constraints, miner/hw/hashboards, miner/stats}, all requiring a Bearer token obtained via POST /api/v1/auth/login (401 on every other endpoint without one) — so 'Auth: Required' should reliably detect on real BOS+ devices. Dropped the overly-generic 'bos+' body pattern too. Not verified against a live device, and plain (non-+) Braiins OS may differ — flag if this doesn't match in practice.

miners.note: cgminer RPC API on port 4028 — plaintext, no auth by default

PARTIALLY UNVERIFIED: checked the real vnish backend source (256foundation/asic-rs) and its actual API base path is /api/v1/{command} with Bearer-token auth via /api/v1/unlock — '/api/summary' (no /v1/, and 'summary' isn't a command name I could find in that source) looks wrong, but I couldn't pin down the exact read-only status command to replace it with confidently. Title/body patterns look fine (vendor name is specific). Treat auth_indicator_path/firmware_extraction here as unreliable until checked against a live device.

UNVERIFIED: couldn't confirm /api/status against any authoritative source — the real luxminer backend in 256foundation/asic-rs only implements the cgminer RPC protocol on port 4028/3333, not an HTTP API, so this path may not exist at all. Dropped bare 'Luxor'/'luxor' from both title and body patterns (2026-07-20) — a GitHub search turned up real, unrelated hits (a Julia graphics library, a museum page, an unrelated DeFi frontend) confirming it's exactly the same class of generic-word false-positive as the Avalon/deviceModel/ESP32 fixes elsewhere in this file. I'd previously written here that the vendor-name patterns were 'likely fine regardless' without actually checking — same mistake as the EdgeOS claim, don't repeat it.

firmware_extraction.note: Verified against a live device: /api/system (no suffix) and /api/miner both 404 on real AxeOS firmware, and the JSON has no 'firmwareVersion' field — the real fields are 'version'/'axeOSVersion'/'idfVersion'/'boardVersion' on /api/system/info. In practice the 'axeos' signature below will usually win ties for current firmware anyway, since it titles itself 'AxeOS' rather than 'Bitaxe' — this entry mainly matters for older firmware or body-only matches.
ESP-Miner firmware, shared codebase with Bitaxe/AxeOS. Same path/field fix as the bitaxe signature — verified against a live device.

Catch-all for community ESP32-based OSMU miners. Same path/field fix as bitaxe/nerdqaxe — /api/system (no suffix) 404s and 'firmwareVersion' isn't a real field on live ESP-Miner-derived firmware. Dropped bare 'ESP32' from body_patterns: on its own (title=0, body=2) it clears the >=2 match threshold, and ESP32 is an extremely common chip in unrelated IoT gear (smart plugs, sensors) that could mention it in their own page body — a real false-positive risk, not just a theoretical one (same class of concern as generic router/switch web panels false-flagging).

miners.note: Catch-all signature for unrecognized ESP-Miner derivatives. Will only match if more specific Bitaxe/NerdQaxe patterns don't trigger first.

benjamin-wilson/public-pool (Angular UI + NestJS backend) and rebranded forks like ClimaxPool. Stock raw HTML title is 'Public Pool'; the Angular SPA body is otherwise near-empty server-side (<app-root></app-root>), so body_patterns rarely match unless a fork hand-edited index.html — the title is the reliable signal here. auth_indicator_path is genuinely null: the stock backend has no browser-facing auth anywhere (only an optional X-API-Key header on the external share-submission endpoint), so 'Auth: Unknown' is the honest answer for this software, not a missing check.

firmware_extraction.note: AxeOS exposes version via /api/system/info JSON. Field: axeOSVersion

Nerd Dashboard exposes version via /api/system/info JSON. Field: version. Dropped 'deviceModel' from body_patterns (2026-07-20) — confirmed false-positive: a colleague's EdgeOS (Ubiquiti) router matched on this alone (body-only score=2 clears the threshold), because EdgeOS's own admin JS config happens to reference 'DeviceModel' too. Generic JSON/JS field names are exactly the wrong kind of pattern here — verify any new one isn't a common naming convention outside mining before adding it back.
